nodes.title: Note:
widgets_values:
USE LLM PROMPT ENHANCE
When enabled, use the LLM Prompt Enhance feature to enhance your prompt.

ENABLE PROMPT MASKING
Automatically generates masks in the image using SAM3. 

PROMPT MASKING
To prompt multiple subjects separately, use commas to separate prompts and specify the maximum number of objects to detect for each prompt using the :N syntax.

For example above test prompt finds 2 cakes, one apron, 4 window panels

```
eye:2, window panels:4
```

USE LLM AUTOMATIC MASKING
When enabled, automatically generate masks using the LLM. If not empty, it can use your manual prompt masking as guidance for elements to mask.

widgets_values.lora: FLUX_KLEIN\realistic-Turn xxx image into a realistic photograph.safetensors

widgets_values: Transform the image to comic style, and ensure there is no text present in the output.

You are a FLUX.2 [klein] image-editing prompt writer.

You receive:

a source image
a raw user instruction

Rewrite the instruction into a clear and effective FLUX.2 editing prompt.

Rules:

Output only the final prompt.
Output in English.
Preserve the user's intent exactly.
Rewrite for clarity, not for creativity.
Keep the requested edit explicit and unambiguous.
Preserve edit actions such as change, replace, remove, add, match, swap, move, resize, recolor, restore, or transform.
Do not summarize the instruction into keywords, tags, labels, or short noun phrases.
Do not invent new subjects, objects, environments, poses, clothing, lighting, camera settings, styles, moods, or details that were not requested.
Assume all unmentioned image content should remain unchanged.
When the edit affects only part of the image, explicitly preserve the rest of the image.
Prefer complete editing instructions over descriptions of the final result.
Keep quoted text and hexadecimal color codes exactly as provided.

Raw user instruction:

Look at the image and list the distinct subjects/objects to segment.

If STRICT_FILTER is not empty, you must interpret it into two lists:
- INCLUDE list (what to keep)
- EXCLUDE list (what to remove)

STRICT_FILTER is always mandatory and overrides all other instructions.

RULE:
1. First convert STRICT_FILTER into INCLUDE and EXCLUDE categories.
2. Then only output objects that are in INCLUDE and NOT in EXCLUDE.
3. If uncertain, exclude the object.

Output ONLY lowercase nouns separated by commas, no sentences, no explanation, no final period. Max 32 tokens total. If several instances of the same thing exist, append :N with N the count (e.g. 'window panel:4, cake:2').

ORDER them from MOST important and LARGEST to least important and smallest, foreground first.

Do NOT include the background, sky or empty areas as an item.

If no matching objects exist, output "none".

Example: person, dog, hat, window panel:3

STRICT_FILTER:

Max 32 tokens total. If several instances of the same thing exist, append :N with N the count (e.g. 'window panel:4, cake:2'). ORDER them from MOST important and LARGEST to least important and smallest, foreground first. Do NOT include the background, sky or empty areas as an item. Example: person, dog, hat, window panel:3

nodes.title: Note: Prompt format
widgets_values:
Max tokens for this model is only 32, to separately prompt multiple subjects you can separate prompts with comma, and set the max amount of objects detected for each prompt with :N

For example above test prompt finds 2 cakes, one apron, 4 window panels
